messages.content:
You are a GDPR compliance analyst. When given a document excerpt, evaluate it step by step against each GDPR requirement. For each step, quote the relevant text if found, or state it is missing. End with a structured verdict listing all violations. Always follow this exact format:

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): ...
Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): ...
Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): ...
Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): ...
Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): ...
Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): ...
Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): ...
Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): ...
Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): ...
Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): ...

VERDICT: [Compliant / Non-Compliant / Partially Compliant]
VIOLATIONS: [list each article breached]
RECOMMENDATIONS: [one line per fix needed]

Assess the following funeral services provider privacy notice for GDPR compliance:

"MemorialCare collects the name, address, and contact details of the person arranging the funeral, and the name, date of death, and cause of death of the deceased. We process this information to provide funeral services and comply with legal registration requirements. Cause of death data is collected as required by the Births, Deaths and Marriages Registration Act. Records are retained for 7 years. You may access or correct your data by contacting our office. We notify the ICO within 72 hours of a breach."

Step 1 — Lawful basis (Art. 6): [PASS] Legal obligation (Art. 6(1)(c)) for death registration and contract (Art. 6(1)(b)) for funeral service delivery both implied and appropriate.

Step 2 — Special category data (Art. 9): [PARTIAL] Cause of death is health data — special category data under Art. 9. Legal obligation basis (Art. 9(2)(g) or national law) is implied by the legislative reference but not explicitly stated as the Art. 9 basis.

Step 3 — Purpose limitation (Art. 5): [PASS] Funeral service delivery and legal registration are clear and separately justified purposes.

Step 4 — Data minimization (Art. 5): [PASS] Data collected is standard and legally required.

Step 5 — Retention period (Art. 5): [PASS] 7-year retention clearly stated.

Step 6 — Data subject rights (Art. 15-21): [PARTIAL] Access and rectification mentioned. Other rights absent. Rights of the living person arranging the funeral should be fully stated.

Step 7 — Third-party disclosure (Art. 28): [PARTIAL] Legal registration with government bodies is implied but not explicitly stated. No mention of cremation or burial site providers as processors.

Step 8 — International transfers (Art. 46): [PASS] Not applicable based on information provided.

Step 9 — DPO appointment (Art. 37): [FAIL] Not addressed.

Step 10 — Breach notification (Art. 33): [PASS] 72-hour ICO notification stated.

VERDICT: Partially Compliant
VIOLATIONS: Art. 9 (explicit Art. 9(2) basis for cause of death data not stated), Art. 15-21 (most rights absent for living data subjects), Art. 37 (DPO not addressed)
RECOMMENDATIONS:
- Explicitly state Art. 9(2) basis for cause of death processing (legal obligation under national law)
- Add full data subject rights section for living individuals arranging funerals
- Disclose any third-party providers involved in service delivery as processors
- Confirm DPO requirement and disclose contact if applicable
